llm_result.summary: This plan targets the case's primary modifiable predictors of task initiation, distraction, time perception, self-criticism, and energy regulation by integrating nine BIO/PSYCHO/SOCIAL levers. The levers span sleep, physical activity, nutrition, environmental design, cognitive restructuring, social accountability, and digital tools to reduce initiation barriers, curb distracting substitutes, improve time-tracking, and lower deadline-related anxiety, without relying on pharmacological strategies. Each lever is actionable, measurable, and designed to produce synergistic effects on C01 (initiation), C02 (substitution with low-effort activities), C03 (distraction/attention), C04 (time-perception), and C06 (self-blame/self-regulation). The approach emphasizes feasibility, safety, and scalable monitoring over 4–8 weeks.

solutions.solution_candidate: Develop explicit if-then action plans (e.g., If seated to work, then start the task within 5 minutes) and schedule tasks in fixed time blocks with start cues.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduction of initiation barrier via precommitment and automatic cue to start; diminishes avoidance and decision paralysis.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns. If panic worsens, adjust task load and provide flexibility.

solutions.solution_candidate: Create a distraction-minimized work space: turn off nonessential notifications, use website/app blockers during focused blocks, and implement a dedicated 'work zone' physically.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Limits automatic attention capture by digital stimuli, promoting sustained attention during planned work periods.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns. If user experiences withdrawal from devices, adjust approach gradually.

solutions.solution_candidate: Use timed work intervals (e.g., Pomodoro) with brief breaks and track actual time spent on the intended task; practice time estimation and correction.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhances time-awareness and reduces underestimation by external time cues and systematic logging.

solutions.solution_candidate: Provide brief CBT-based reframing to distinguish laziness from modifiable difficulties and teach self-compassion scripts to reduce harsh self-judgment.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Alters appraisal patterns, reducing self-blame and increasing willingness to engage in corrective steps.

solutions.solution_candidate: Establish consistent sleep/wake times, optimize pre-sleep routines, and light/dark exposure to improve sleep quality and daytime cognitive control.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep improves attention, self-regulation, and task initiation capacity.

solutions.solution_candidate: Incorporate short daily aerobic activity (e.g., 15-20 minutes brisk walk) to boost executive function and reduce distractibility.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Physical activity improves prefrontal control and attention, aiding task initiation and sustained focus.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; adjust intensity if physical limitations.

solutions.solution_candidate: Maintain steady hydration and balanced meals to stabilize energy and cognitive function throughout the day.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Energy availability supports task initiation/attention and reduces impulsive substitution with low-effort activities.

solutions.solution_candidate: Create a small accountability group or buddy system to check in on task initiation and progress weekly.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social accountability increases commitment, reduces self-blame by shared challenge, and supports initiation.

solutions.solution_candidate: Adopt a digital task management system (calendar + to-do list with visual cues) to offload cognitive load and provide start cues.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Externalizing tasks reduces cognitive load, improves initiation, and reduces susceptibility to distraction with external reminders.
